$comment: This $id is a versioned, immutable release URL (git tag v2.9.0). The same path under 'main' always carries the latest published schema. Pin the tagged URL in documents; see skel-spec.md §8 and GOVERNANCE.md for the release policy.
title: SKEL — Skeleton
description: A flat, relational SKEL data model for representing visual narratives. Native files are YAML; JSON is used for validation and export.

skel_version.description: Spec version (semver). MAJOR.MINOR required; the PATCH component MAY be included (e.g. "2.0" or "2.0.1"). Parsers MUST reject unsupported major versions.

bone_registry.description: Embedded BONE entries keyed by bone_id. May hold either the full canonical BONE definition (portable, self-contained) or a slim subset that resolves the rest from templates/bones or spec/bones at runtime.

characters.description: Optional embedded character snapshots referenced by shots' character_refs. Primary reference is by id into the studio registry (studio.json, see studio.schema.json); embed on lifecycle: export for a portable, self-contained file.

environments.description: Optional embedded environment snapshots referenced by scenes' environment_ref. An environment is a dressed/lit variant of a location. Primary reference is by id (studio.json); embed on export.

locations.description: Optional embedded location snapshots referenced by scenes' location_ref and environments' location_ref. A location is the physical place; environments are its dressed/lit variants. Primary reference is by id (studio.json); embed on export.

props.description: Optional embedded prop snapshots referenced by shots' prop_refs and characters' props_carried. First-class continuity objects. Primary reference is by id (studio.json); embed on export.

audio_assets.description: Optional embedded audio asset snapshots. Primary reference is via shots' sound_effects / music_cues / audio-map.json; embed on export.

music_cues.description: Optional document-level music/score cues that span shots. Each cue references an audio asset and in/out points anchored to shots. Per-shot assignment stays in sound_effects and the audio-map sidecar; music_cues is the score lane.

key_file.description: Inline key file object or URI to external key file.

Extensions.description: Vendor-namespaced extension data. Keys MUST start with 'x-' (enforced via propertyNames).

Bones.description: BONE data keyed by bone_id. Values are open objects defined by the BONE definition.

BoneRegistryEntry.description: Embedded form of a BONE in story.skel's bone_registry. Only the minimum needed for runtime resolution is required; full canonical fields (bone_version, attaches_to, per-field 'required') are accepted but not mandatory.

provider.description: Optional provider namespace, such as higgsfield, runway, kling, or flux.

llm_instructions.description: Generator-specific writing guidance for LLM prompt authors.

output.description: Render output format, target, storage template, and write-back behavior.

routing.description: Optional provider routing metadata or lookup hints. Projects may also keep routing in external config.

execution_routes.description: Optional executable routes the agent can choose from, such as MCP, CLI, or skill package routes.

extensions.description: Vendor-specific extension data. Keys should be x- namespaced.

requires.description: Capability ids this BONE needs in order to fire (host capability matrix).

pipeline_stage.description: Optional pipeline stage this BONE participates in (e.g. proposal, scene_plan, generation, review).

authoring_mode.description: Templated, Atelier/Bespoke, or Hybrid authoring. Absence = unset.

render_runtime.description: Preferred composition runtime. Governance/selector metadata only.

decision_log_refs.description: IDs of host decision-log entries that motivated or constrain this BONE.

quality_gates.description: Optional quality gates this BONE must satisfy — governance metadata for host reviewer surfaces.

QualityGate.description: A quality gate a BONE declares it must satisfy, shaped like a reviewer finding. Runtime-only fields (id/timestamp) are optional because a BONE declares gates rather than emitting findings.

PromptAssembly.description: Prompt assembly strategy and context injection rules.

template.description: Template string using {{field_name}} and supported system tokens.

max_length.description: Maximum assembled prompt length in characters.

separator.description: Separator used by sequential assembly. Defaults to a single space.

CharacterInjection.description: Controls injection of character data from character_refs. Format 'consistency_modifier' MUST emit each referenced character's identity_lock text verbatim.

lifecycle.description: Document lifecycle mode. Draft allows empty or partial structure; production/export apply stricter validation.

target_duration_seconds.description: Target runtime in seconds. Canonical duration field: when both duration fields are present, target_duration_seconds wins and validators SHOULD warn (DURATION_CONFLICT) if the two disagree by more than one second.

target_duration_minutes.description: Target runtime in minutes. Derived display convenience only; target_duration_seconds is canonical.

status.description: Free-form production status label. Distinct from per-entity status.image/status.video.

language.description: Project default language as a BCP 47 tag (e.g. en, en-US, pt-BR). Per-line overrides live on Dialogue.lang.

skin_key.description: Reference to a visual style/skin preset. Resolves against the studio registry's skins collection (studio.schema.json).

plugins.description: Host-owned record of MUSCLE behavior plugins whose patches were applied to this document. Reproducibility metadata only — a document with unknown plugin records must still load everywhere.

SeriesRef.description: Marks this document as one episode of a series. The series document itself (seasons, episode index, arcs, shared cast) lives in the studio registry (studio.schema.json).

arc_refs.description: Season/series arc IDs from the series document that this episode advances.

previously.description: story_ids of episodes whose events this episode assumes.

Delivery.description: Project delivery facts: frame rate, resolution, aspect, color, and audio targets. Required for timecode math, OTIO/NLE export, and render pipelines.

frame_rate.description: Frames per second; the timecode base (e.g. 24, 23.976, 25, 30).

aspect.description: Project default aspect ratio (e.g. "2.39:1"). v_setup.aspect overrides per shot.

loudness_target_lufs.description: Integrated loudness target, e.g. -14 (streaming) or -23 (EBU R128).

SourceProvenance.description: Where this document was imported from. Enables lossless round-trips: exporters back to the source format restore data parked under the matching x-<format> extension namespace.

format.description: Source format id: fountain, fdx, otio, skel.json, csv, ...

file.description: Original source file name or path.

order.description: Derived display index. The refs arrays (and the acts array order) are canonical; validators SHOULD warn (ORDER_MISMATCH) when order fields disagree with ref-array order.

Loc.description: Parsed location block. loc is canonical; the scene header string is derived display text and validators SHOULD warn (HEADER_LOC_MISMATCH) when the two disagree.

tod.description: Time-of-day token (see key file). CONT means continuous with the previous scene — previous by act order, then scene_refs order; validators SHOULD warn (TOD_CONT_FIRST_SCENE) when the first scene uses CONT. SAME means the same time as the previous scene after a cutaway; LATER means an unspecified jump forward.

CreativeStatus.description: Story-development status for a scene or shot. Distinct from production status (status.image / status.video). 'locked' indicates creative content should not be modified by AI rewrites or automated edits.

SceneIntent.description: Optional creative purpose and structural function of the scene. Used by LLMs and directors to understand why a scene exists, not just what happens in it.

purpose.description: What this scene reveals, establishes, or accomplishes for the story.

conflict.description: The dramatic tension or opposition driving this scene.

emotional_turn.description: How the emotional state or audience feeling changes from the scene's start to its end.

story_function.description: The structural role this scene plays in the narrative arc.

ShotIntent.description: Optional creative purpose and emphasis for a single shot. LLMs MUST preserve intent when rewriting action or prompt text.

beat.description: The specific story beat or moment this shot captures.

function.description: The structural role this shot plays within its scene.

emphasis.description: What the shot should make the audience feel or notice. Used by LLMs when rewriting action or prompt text.

order.description: Derived display index. The refs arrays are canonical ordering; validators SHOULD warn (ORDER_MISMATCH) on disagreement.

header.description: Screenplay slug line for display (e.g. INT. LIGHTHOUSE - NIGHT). Derived from loc, which is canonical.

location_ref.description: Reference to the physical place (Location). A location is the place itself; an environment is a dressed/lit variant of it.

environment_ref.description: Reference to the dressed/lit variant of the location (Environment) this scene plays in.

story_time.description: Sortable in-world chronological position: an ISO timestamp, a day number, or an ordinal. Presentation order (acts/scene_refs) is separate from story chronology; this is the chronology axis.

time_elapsed_since_previous.description: Display string for the in-world time gap since the previous scene in presentation order (e.g. "three days later").

narrative_mode.description: How this scene relates to the story's present timeline. Default when omitted: present.

transition_out.description: Editorial transition out of this scene (see key file transition category). Omitted implies a straight cut.

VSetup.description: Visual setup tokens. Every token field accepts either a core enum value or an x- prefixed custom token declared in the key file's custom section with a matching category.

Status.description: Per-shot media production status. This core field is the canonical home of production status; extensions.x-genlock.production_status is a deprecated mirror (see MIGRATIONS.md).

order.description: Derived display index. The refs arrays are canonical ordering; validators SHOULD warn (ORDER_MISMATCH) on disagreement.

dialogue.description: A plain spoken line (string), a structured dialogue object, or an ordered array of Dialogue objects for multi-line exchanges within one shot.

prop_refs.description: IDs of props visible or used in this shot. Enables machine-checkable prop continuity.

transition_out.description: Editorial transition out of this shot (see key file transition category). Omitted implies a straight cut.

audio_description.description: Optional described-video text for accessibility: what a non-sighted audience needs narrated for this shot.

Dialogue.description: Structured dialogue: the spoken line plus timing, emotion, and voice direction. character_ref SHOULD be present when the shot lists more than one character (validators warn DIALOGUE_AMBIGUOUS_SPEAKER).

mode.description: Delivery mode: on-screen spoken (default), voice-over (V.O.), off-screen (O.S.), inner thought, sung, or through an in-world device/radio.

lang.description: BCP 47 language tag for this line. Defaults to metadata.language.

subtitle.description: Optional display/subtitle text when it differs from the spoken text (translation, stylization).

SoundCue.description: A per-shot reference to an audio asset with placement. For score/music spanning multiple shots use the document-level music_cues collection.

MusicCue.description: A music/score cue spanning one or more shots. in/out anchor to shots by ID with optional offsets so a single cue can cross scene boundaries without duplication.

audio_ref.description: Audio asset ID. SHOULD resolve to audio_assets when embedded, or to the studio registry.

out.description: Optional end anchor. Omitted means the cue runs to the end of its audio or the end of the timeline, whichever comes first.

shot_ref.description: Shot ID this point anchors to. MUST resolve to a shot.

offset_seconds.description: Offset from the start of the anchored shot. Defaults to 0. Negative values lead the cut.

Cinematography.description: Optional verbose camera + lighting detail for a shot. v_setup remains the canonical normalized (shorthand-token) form.

Character.description: A character definition. Embedded in .skel documents as a snapshot (export/portable) and stored canonically in the studio registry (studio.schema.json). additionalProperties stays open for backward compatibility with pre-2.9 loose snapshots; new vendor fields SHOULD use extensions.

age_range.description: e.g. "55-65". Preferred over exact age for casting/generation latitude.

identity_lock.description: THE canonical 30-60-word appearance description. This exact text is what the character-reference-sheet BONE consumes and every downstream prompt (storyboard, video) MUST reuse verbatim. CharacterInjection format 'consistency_modifier' emits this field.

wardrobe_variants.description: Costume variants with the scenes they apply to. The costume-continuity backbone.

reference_sheet.description: Path to the rendered character reference sheet image.

identity_refs.description: Machine identity anchors for generation pipelines.

want.description: External goal: what the character pursues.

need.description: Internal truth: what the character actually requires.

first_appearance.description: Shot ID of the character's first appearance.

props_carried.description: Prop IDs habitually carried by this character.

state_overrides.description: Per-scene continuity state: wardrobe, injuries, notes.

scene_refs.description: Scenes in which this wardrobe variant is worn.

casting.description: Casting note or voice reference ("in the register of ...").

voice_settings.description: TTS provider settings (provider, voice_id, stability, ...). Feeds TTS BONEs; per-line overrides live on Dialogue.voice_settings.

type.description: Free-form relationship type: estranged_son, mentor, rival, ...

wardrobe_ref.description: WardrobeVariant id in effect for this scene.

injuries.description: Visible injury/damage state carried into this scene.

Environment.description: A dressed/lit variant of a location (e.g. lighthouse-interior-storm-night). Scenes point at environments via environment_ref; environments point at their physical place via location_ref. additionalProperties stays open for backward compatibility with pre-2.9 loose snapshots.

location_ref.description: The parent Location this environment dresses.

style_lock.description: The canonical prompt text for this environment, mirroring Character.identity_lock: reused verbatim by downstream prompts for set consistency.

geography.description: Spatial layout notes: what is left of the door, where the window faces. The thing AI shot continuity always breaks without.

tod_variants.description: Per-time-of-day lighting/description variants; the same room at NIGHT is a different prompt than at DAWN.

soundscape_refs.description: Audio asset IDs for ambient beds belonging to this environment.

props_present.description: Prop IDs dressed into this environment.

Location.description: A physical place (one lighthouse). Environments are its dressed/lit variants; scenes reference both.

Prop.description: A first-class continuity object. significance: plot_critical props are machine-checkable: validators SHOULD warn (PROP_CONTINUITY) when a plot-critical prop appears once and never again.

first_appearance.description: Shot ID where the prop first appears.

carried_by.description: Character ID that habitually carries this prop.

StoryAnalysis.description: Optional, informational story-development analysis. Does not affect validation strictness or generation. The properties below are the RECOMMENDED shape so tools converge; the object stays open.

emotional_curve.description: Per-scene emotional intensity samples in presentation order.

Production.description: Optional, informational production planning + tracking. Tool-managed; not validated strictly. The properties below are the RECOMMENDED shape so tools converge; the object stays open.

budget.description: Budget tracking; per-BONE generation cost estimates belong here.

CustomKeyFileToken.description: A vendor-registered custom token. The x- prefixed token validates in the schema's token fields; category declares which token field it belongs to.
